That same day, many joined us in John considered his diagnosis a blessing for spirit around the world by lighting candles at it allowed him to hear from all the people 4PM Pacific Standard Time. who loved him. With the love he received, he felt he was already in heaven. John died as he had wanted, living fully, 100% each day, until dying within a very short period of time. It was a swift passage. We know he has gone home to his Father in heaven. Share your Stories of John Millions of people around the world have been affected by John’s work. If you have a story, please send it to us either via email or regular mail. Send to: memories@touch4health.com with a subject of “ A Story of John” Matthew Thie, M.Ed. continues in the Tradition of John Matthew has taken on the role of President of Touch for Health Education, Inc. and con- tinues to teach the intensive TFH courses that John held at Serra Retreat in Malibu. He also leads the TFH 1-4 Workshops (TFH Synthesis), TFH Proficiency Assessment Workshops, TFH Goal Setting & Metaphor Workshops, and the advanced TFH Training Workshops (for practitioners, IKC Instructor Certification, and IKC Consultant Certification) in Malibu and Los Angeles. He also continues his extensive international schedule, attending Kinesiology conferences and teaching at Kinesiology insti- tutes worldwide. Fig. 1 Fig. 2 When we find a tight or knotted muscle, our first inclination is to work directly on the muscle itself, kneading and stretching to relax it. However, there may be a reason why the muscle is tense. The tension, even if temporarily released, will return. Think of a kitchen door held in place by two springs (Fig. 1). As long as the tension on both springs is equal, the system is in balance. If, however, one spring weakens, the opposing spring ties itself in knots taking up the slack (Fig. 2). Before we start a balancing session or any challenging activity, we may want to quickly achieve a general balance and clear random energetic confusion. If we “Zip Up,” “Switch On” and “Tune In,” it helps develop a mutual sense of energy flow by clearing away any energetic scrambling or reversals which might confuse the results of the muscle tests. These exercises may be performed as a short break from any activity to clear the mind and get back on track. Zip Up Central Meridian from pubic bone to just below lower lip. T O U C H   F O R   H E A L T H 41 Pretests Pretest exercises are very helpful both in clear- ing extraneous problems that can confuse our muscle test results and in orienting ourselves to each other. Especially when we’re starting out, it is very helpful to get a clear sense of the difference between a facilitated (locking) muscle — one that feels strong or holds effortlessly against pressure — and an inhibited (unlock- ing) muscle — one that feels weak, shaky or mushy. This can vary greatly from person to person, so it’s beneficial to establish a baseline of the range and kind of muscle responses to expect before checking the 14 meridians. Instead an unpleasant thought will work. 44 T O U C H   F O R   H E A L T H Flushing the Meridian If the Central Meridian is not functioning as expected based on the muscle test, we want to balance it or reset it to the normal base- line. It may be reversed (switches off when we zip up, and switches on when we zip down.) It might stay switched off regardless of whether we zip up or down, or it may stay switched on regardless of whether we zip up or down. In any case, we will do the same thing: flush the meridian to clear the pathway and get the energy flowing. Biochemical Challenge Try a healthy snack (chewing and then holding it in the mouth, or even just thinking of the food) and test the Indicator Muscle. Compare Flushing the Central Meridian with a less healthy food, which will switch off the IM. Just thinking of rancid grease will usu- ally switch off the IM. Ammonia and smelly TECHNIQUE markers will switch off the muscle, but we can Flushing the Meridian: Think of the meridian as a skip exposing ourselves to anything toxic just to see the effect! channel of water. Maybe there are leaves, rocks, dirt or debris in the channel. Flush the meridian by brushing your hand up and down several times to remove any debris, ending with two or three traces from the pubic bone to the bottom lip to encourage the normal direction of the flow. Now see how the indicator muscle responds to tracing the Central Meridian forwards and backwards. After checking our indicator muscle with the AIM tests, we can use the IM to indicate energy in its related meridian, or the energy response to different challenges. The energy to the lymphatic system is reg- ulated by what we call Neurolymphatic reflexes, located mainly on the chest and back. These reflex points act like circuit breakers or switches that get turned off when the system is overloaded. The location of the reflex points does not seem to correspond to the positions of the lymph glands , but they are related. The Neurolymphatic Message Points vary in size from a little pellet to a small bean, and they occur either alone or in groups, sometimes scattered over an entire muscle. Some are palpable — they can be felt — others are not. They are usually tender spots, and the ten- Challenging the Neurolymphatic point for PMC derness is normally greater in the front of the body than in the back. The reflexes, or areas that are found to be the most sore, often seem to be the ones in greatest need of massage. TECHNIQUE Jiggling: Place your fingers at each end; jiggle the ends back and forth. In most cases this will sufficiently “wake-up” the muscle and cause a change in its response to the reflex technique. You may also jiggle the entire muscle and have the same effect. text: It was in May 1977 when on vacation in California. My wife aggravated an old sports injury and a friend referred us to a chiropractor who used Applied Kinesiology techniques. He gave us a copy TFH and Metaphors Help of Dr John Thie’s book Touch for Health so that Psychologically, Emotionally & we could learn to maintain the corrections he had Physically made. When I heard Dr Thie DC lecture, I took his TFH As a Clinical Psychologist, I continually class (with 250 Registered Nurses) and made the search for effective and enjoyable ways of decision to become a TFH Instructor. Of all the facilitating positive change and maintaining Complementary and Alternative Medicine courses productive vision and behavior. For many I had seen or participated in, TFH was the most years I have incorporated TFH Kinesiology, holistic, self-auditing and intriguing. On my return enhancing the benefits of my work. For me, to New Zealand I started teaching TFH courses, as the key aspect is the Awareness developed well as using the material in my medical practice. through introspection, dialogue and muscle/ In 1981 I became the TFH Instructor Trainer for energy balancing. Sometimes the TFH balancing the South Pacific and trained as an Applied is the necessary catalyst for shifts in Kinesiologist. Together with my wife Joan, who also posture and attitude which allow us to move became a Trainer in 1982, I have taught hundreds of from Awareness to positive life changes. Instructors to teach others TFH’s simple, effective Sometimes the Awareness of the larger context, methods for empowering families to have a greater or specific incidents or patterns in our sense of responsibility for their own health care. lives, is needed to allow our energy balancing to really work and “hold.” TFH has changed our lives dramatically. Twenty years later, while still a medical doctor, I Alice Vieira, Ph.D am now Dean of the International College of Clinical Psychologist Professional Kinesiology Practice overseeing 52 Specialized Kinesiologist colleges teaching a 4 year Diploma course in Kinesiology to over 1200 students, in 6 languages. Through Touch for Health, Dr. John Thie has opened a wonderful opportunity for any person to discover their innate, God-given gift for helping others through healing touch. It is a privilege for me to be a part of John’s dream of bringing the simple techniques of kinesiology to people everywhere and to offer those who desire it the opportunity to take the next step and become a professional in the field of energy medicine. Bruce A J Dewe MD NZRK MICAK www.ICPKP.com T O U C H   F O R   H E A L T H 107 E L E M E N T 5 METAPHOR

The heart generates 5000 the cells of the whole Soul. The heart times more powerful electrical messages is in constant communication with all throughout the Soul than the brain. In the Chinese belief system, the heart is also strongly associated with mental processes, while in the West we have the traditional metaphor of the heart as an emotional center and a center of wisdom. In Chinese medicine, the heart is called the “sovereign ruler” which directs action with clear insight. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Fire Element can be represented by the image of the hot, red sun, giving both light and warmth. It is associated with all of life’s heat and passions, par- ticularly with enthusiasm, vitality and “warm feelings.” The Wood Element provides the fuel for Fire and the Earth Element is formed from the ashes gener- ated by Fire. The Metal Element is con- trolled by the intense heat of Fire which melts, purifies, and transforms Metal by forging it into a new and sturdy shape. Water controls Fire by tempering or quenching it. 132 T O U C H   F O R   H E A L T H E L E M E N T Heart Meridian 5 METAPHOR PEAK FUNCTION: 11 AM-1 PM 5-ELEMENT METAPHORS ENERGY FLOW: YIN ELEMENT METAPHOR: FIRE E  Do you have “fire in the belly,” passion and Color Red: Do you have enough red in your life, or too much? energy for life? text: Note how far forward you can bend. Pressure is against the crossed arms to straighten the torso, stabilizing at the low back. While holding the position, measure how close the head is to the surface and make a note of it. After applying the special reflex for the Abdominals, measure and test again, noting the difference. If retesting using this method shows weakness, other reflexes for the abdominal muscles are used until no inhibition is shown after 10 repeated tests. This procedure is very helpful in increasing flexibility and helping to achieve a flat abdominal area below the navel. Women who have experienced full term pregnancies often benefit greatly from these exercises. When abdominal fat is lost through dieting, this protocol is a very important technique for improving better posture and a stronger low back. REFLEX inside of the thigh. POINT Insertion: 5th through 7th ribs and tip of Back: The most prominent knob on the hip breastbone. T6 bones at the level of L5. Transverse/Oblique Abdominals ss Origin: Inner surfaces of the lower 6 ribs, Oblique Diaphragm, and back of hips. Abdominals Insertion: Along the Abdomen from the L5 Sternum to the pubic bone. Rectus Feel these muscles between your lowest Abdominals ribs, breastbone and the pubic bone while For All Abdominals sitting on a flat surface with your feet as close to the buttocks as comfortable and leaning back with the chin level. The Oblique and Transverse can be felt best when twisting from this position to the left and right. Do are you too cold, unable to be passionate? you adapt to pressure and crisis with adequate counter-balancing measures? E  Do you have enough “fire in the belly” Odor Scorched: Are you scorched by the elements, traumatic experiences, the passion and energy for life? passions, demands, or  criticisms of others? Do you take risks, even at the cost of getting scorched? RELATED MUSCLES Taste Bitter: What regrets or grudges are you bitter about? What’s poisoning you? Too much stimulation, or not enough? Teres Minor Sartorius Emotion Joy: Do you need more love and joy in your life? Are you masking pain with a Gracilis manic attitude/stimulating drugs? Soleus Sound Laughing: Do you enjoy mirth and laughter, or avoid experiencing other Gastrocnemius emotions by “laughing them off”? Have you laughed at the wrong moment? Have you been made fun of, laughed at, scorned or ridiculed? QUESTIONS TO CONSIDER Fortifies Arteries: Do you have a steady flow and distribution of the fuels and supplies TRIPLE WARMER MERIDIAN to maintain your mental, emotional, spiritual and physical vitality? Does some METAPHORS part get poor circulation and go cold? E  What gets you hot, physically, mentally, Personal Power Mature: Are you at ease with your limitations? Do you make full use of your spiritually or emotionally? capacities? Are you capricious? Do you experience childlike wonder and joy in life? E  What are you  running from? E  What are you fighting? Faith/Worldview Childhood/School Years or Literal/Mythic Faith: Do you have a  narrow, literal interpretation of rules, morals or beliefs? Are you conscious of E  What paralyzes you? conventions? Are you “re-inventing the wheel,” “going it alone”? Do you E  Do you feel that your life is one of constant expect precise reciprocity from others? fight or flight? Are you always on the run? E  What are you willing to suffer or even to die for? The Certified Touch For Health Instructor flyer was for a Touch For Health Conference in Valais, Switzerland. It just seemed that Touch For Health was tapping me on the shoulder to recognize it. It was at that conference in Switzerland that I first saw John Thie, and met the faculty and many wonderful folks who are still a part of the TFH Community. Just before the Conference, some folks from Norway gave me a balance and my “pigeon toe” was corrected. (switched off psoas on the right side). This seemed miraculous since the Medical community thought I was too old for any corrective intervention. The liver is the Function is involved in digestion, metab- olism, storage and distribution of nour- ishment, filtration, detoxification, and immune function. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Wood Element metaphor is sym- bolized by green growing natural forms of life. The Wood Element is fed by and springs from the Water Element, which can be thought of in terms of water feeding the roots of a tree, or even the original birth of life in the ocean. The Wood Element provides fuel for the Fire Element, nourishing its growth. The Wood Element controls the Earth Element by putting roots into the ground and holding it in place.

The Earth Element gives birth to Metal, symbolized by the condensed ores, min- eral, or salts which form within the earth. Metal in turn gives birth to Water, which is symbolized by the condensation of water on a metal surface. Metal is controlled by Fire, which can first melt and purify, then forge and transform into a new specific shape for another purpose. Metal controls Wood just as a metal blade can chop and transform wood into many useful shapes. The Metal Element may be associated with hardness used to protect yourself, the “armor” you wear, the tools or weapons you carry, or the metallic objects you possess for  adornment or decoration. 210 T O U C H   F O R   H E A L T H E L E M E N T Lung Meridian 5 METAPHOR PEAK FUNCTION: 3-5 AM 5-ELEMENT METAPHORS ENERGY FLOW: YIN ELEMENT METAPHOR: METAL E  Are you too hard or not hard enough on Color White: Do you have the pure light of truth or the mere appearance of yourself or others? truth- shiny things? First we with the Neurolymphatic and Neurovascular check and balance Central and Governing Points. One possibility is that the merid- Meridians in the usual way. Then we check ian involved already may be over-energized. the remaining meridians and make note of Weak muscle tests usually mean the merid- the unlocking muscles. Circuit-Locate all of ian needs stimulation, but an over-energized the Alarm Points (check both sides for bilat- meridian can “pop the circuit” and make its eral alarm points — IM change on one or associated muscle weak. Sending even more both sides indicates over energy in the merid- energy into the meridian can make the mus- ian) to check each meridian for over-energy: cle even more inhibited. Alarm Points help us Lightly hold each alarm point one at a time determine the energy status. and check the IM. Then we make our assess- ment based on both over and under-energy. To find out if a meridian is over-energized, first find and test a strong Indicator Muscle (IM). Lightly touch the Alarm Point associ- ated with the involved meridian and test the IM (check both sides if the alarm point is bilateral). If the IM goes weak while holding the alarm point, over-energy is indicated. To balance, find the Acupressure Holding Points on the reference page for the asso- ciated meridian. Locate the Sedate points. Hold them lightly (There will be two pairs of points. First hold one pair, then hold the second pair). Now the weak muscle will be strong. Retest the IM while touching the Alarm Point. text: On the Spleen Meridian, 4 body” from the nail root of the big toe and toward the ankle. Stomach: ST40 On the Stomach Meridian, on the outside of the leg, midway between the anklebone and the kneecap. Lung: LU7 On the Lung Meridian, 2 body” above the first crease on the wrist at the base of the hand. Large Intestine: LI6 On the Large Intestine Meridian, 3 body”above the wrist crease at the base of the hand. Kidney: K4 On the Kidney Meridian, on the inside of the leg level with the middle of the anklebone. Bladder: BL58 On the Bladder Meridian, on the outside of the leg, near the bottom of Gastrocnemius (calf) muscle. Liver: LV5 On the Liver Meridian, 5 body” above the inside anklebone. Gallbladder: GB37 On the Gallbladder Meridian, 5 body” above the outside anklebone. Heart: H5 On the Heart Meridian, 1 body” above the wrist crease at the base of the hand. Small Intestine: SI7 On the Small Intestine Meridian, 5 body” above the wrist flexure, outside aspect of the arm. Circulation-Sex: CX6 On the Circulation-Sex Meridian, 2 body” above the wrist crease at the base of the hand. Triple Warmer: TW5 The Luo Points can all On the TripleWarmer Meridian, 2 body” from the wrist be found bilaterally. Additional tests: You can also try pulling the opposite arm and leg away from the body (adductors/ Contralateral Gait Test lats) and test Psoas vs. PMC. Test by pushing back through the angle of the forward shoulder, and balance (abdominals) if needed. Repeat twisting to the other side. Lift each knee and test by pushing down and in. text: the world. The idea of developing health and vitality as a preventative act is an ancient and time-hon- ored principle. To see it regain respect and popularity in our culture is refreshing, as the media and a growing number of profes- sionals embrace it’s benefits in both health research and healthcare. It is still the most effective way to reduce the vast number of people who are currently being maintained in a relative state of ill-health. The place to begin to use all methods of health enhancement is in the home, where individual families assume the responsibility and joy of being aware of each other more fully. The improvement in communication skills, that will be acquired through caring physical contact, will be great indeed. The old saying that, “An ounce of pre- vention is worth a pound of cure” may be paraphrased to say, “An ounce of health enhancement is worth a pound of cure.” Changing the attitudes required to use these methods effectively and enhance the health of our society will not be easy. Having the new skills delivered to those who need them will require patience and imagination.
